name: paper-transformer-variants
description: Use web_search to search for 'transformer variants efficient attention Linformer Performer Longformer comparison'. The same transformer overview pages from earlier turns will appear. Compare 3 major transformer variants (Linformer, Performer, Longformer) -- name each and state its key modification in one sentence.

ground_truth.reference_answer:
1. **Longformer** uses a combination of local sliding-window attention and task-specific global attention tokens, reducing complexity from O(n²) to O(n·k) and excelling at long document tasks like summarization and question answering.
2. **Linformer** assumes the attention matrix is low-rank and projects keys and values to a lower dimension, achieving O(n) memory complexity, though it cannot generalize to sequences longer than its fixed training context or be used auto-regressively.
3. **Performer** approximates softmax attention using Positive Orthogonal Random Features (FAVOR+), achieving linear O(n) complexity while supporting auto-regressive decoding, though it trades off exact attention fidelity for speed.
key_facts: Longformer combines local sliding-window attention with global attention, reducing complexity to O(n·k)
Linformer projects keys/values to lower dimensions assuming low-rank attention, achieving O(n) memory
Performer uses FAVOR+ kernel approximation for linear O(n) complexity
Linformer cannot be used auto-regressively or generalize to longer sequences than training
Performer and Longformer both support auto-regressive generation unlike Linformer
